budget.note: Costs are approximate and may vary by season (High season: March-May, Sept-Nov). Prices are subject to change by local transport and hotel operators.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Common queries for pilgrims and travelers.

questions.q: When is the best time to visit Muktinath?
questions.a: The best time to visit is during the Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) when the weather is clear and the mountain views are at their best. These seasons are ideal for both flights and road travel.

questions.q: Do I need any special permits for this trip?
questions.a: Yes, visitors generally require the Annapurna Conservation Area Project (ACAP) permit and a TIMS (Trekkers' Information Management Systems) card. Requirements vary for domestic and international tourists.

questions.q: How can I prevent altitude sickness?
questions.a: Muktinath is located at 3,710 meters. To stay safe, stay hydrated, avoid rapid ascent, and consider spending a night in Jomsom or Kagbeni to acclimate. Consult a doctor about medication like Diamox if needed.

questions.q: Are there ATMs available in Muktinath?
questions.a: There are limited ATM services in Ranipauwa (near the temple), but they can be unreliable due to connectivity issues. It is highly recommended to carry enough local cash from Pokhara or Jomsom.

questions.q: Is the road to Muktinath suitable for all vehicles?
questions.a: The road from Beni to Jomsom is rugged and often off-road. While high-clearance jeeps and motorcycles are best, local buses also operate. Small cars are not recommended for this route.

travelers.title: Number of Travelers

contextPanel.title: Travel Tips & Info
contextPanel.totalEstimate: Estimated total for {adults} adult(s) and {children} child(ren):
contextPanel.includes: Includes transport, accommodation ({nights} nights), food ({days} days), and permits.

fast.food: Packed breakfast, local thali set (NPR 400-600), dinner at hotel
tips: Book flights at least 2 weeks in advance
Flights often cancel due to weather – keep a buffer day
Carry cash for permits and local expenses

standard.food: Breakfast at lodge, packed lunch, dinner at local restaurant
tips: Start early to avoid afternoon winds on the road
Try local apple brandy in Marpha
Carry warm layers even in summer

family.food: Family thali, continental options, kid-friendly meals
tips: Book private transport for comfort and flexibility
Pack snacks and entertainment for children
Ensure accommodation has attached bathrooms and heating

adventure.food: High-energy trail food, local dal bhat, packed lunches
tips: Carry a basic repair kit for motorcycles
Fuel is scarce – fill up whenever possible
Respect local culture and seek permission before photographing
Acclimatize properly – altitude sickness can hit anyone

budget.food: Local dal bhat (NPR 250-350/meal), noodle soup, chai
tips: Carry enough cash – ATMs in Jomsom are unreliable
Shared jeeps fill up quickly – go early
Negotiate teahouse prices if staying multiple nights
Drink plenty of water and avoid alcohol for acclimatization

permits.description: ACAP NPR {acap} per adult (children under 10 free), TIMS NPR {tims} per adult

bus.title: Travel by Bus (Budget/Solo Plan)
bus.condition: Expect bumpy mountain roads. Highly recommended for budget-conscious travelers and solo backpackers.
bus.tip: This is the most cost-effective way to reach the region [1].

flight.title: Travel by Flight (Fast/Family Plan)
flight.time: 20-minute flight, followed by a 1.5-hour jeep ride to Muktinath [1].
flight.condition: Weather-dependent. Best for the 2-Day Express or Family plans to avoid long road travel [1].
flight.safety: Flights usually operate in the early morning to avoid high winds.

jeep.title: Travel by Jeep (Adventure Plan)
jeep.condition: Rugged Himalayan terrain. You can choose between shared jeeps (budget-friendly) or private jeeps (comfortable) [1].
jeep.recommendation: Ideal for adventure seekers wanting to see the gates of Upper Mustang.
